headline: MARA Scholarship Recipients Face Bonding Disputes Worth RM890 Million
context: Over 3,400 MARA scholarship recipients face legal action or bond disputes over service requirements as of 2025.

cards.big: 3,400 MARA scholars face bond claims totaling RM890 million for unfulfilled service
cards.sub: Many were unable to find positions in designated sectors after graduation

cards.big: MARA bonding requires 5 years domestic service for fully funded overseas degrees
cards.sub: Scholars who cannot find relevant employment still face full bond repayment

cards.big: 42% of bond disputes involve scholars who returned but found no suitable positions
cards.sub: Mismatch between bonded sectors and available jobs creates systemic default

cards.big: Ireland replaced scholarship bonds with voluntary return incentives achieving 71% return rate
cards.sub: Positive incentives outperform punitive bonds in attracting scholars home

cards.big: Punishing scholars for labor market failures they did not create is counterproductive
Should scholarship bonds be waived when graduates demonstrate genuine inability to find bonded roles
